rag_rules: Fix latched expression diagnostics by ensuring they appear only in component implementations and reference valid input ports or event expressions.
Resolve type mismatch errors by aligning helper declarations with their expression types and avoiding invalid references.
Address syntax errors by correcting unmatched parentheses and ensuring binary expressions use valid boolean types.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in appropriate scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Generated AGREE code should only reference visible identifiers and avoid introducing new package or type dependencies.
Use valid AGREE logical operators and syntax patterns for expressing temporal and conditional relationships.
Ensure all AGREE expressions resolve to valid types; avoid <error> types by tracing and fixing root causes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with correct syntax and type alignment.
Latched expressions are only valid in component implementations, not in component types.
Binary expressions like `and` must have boolean operands; use proper type declarations for helpers.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level references or subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or new data types not present in the model.
Do not add architecture or new declarations when resolving AGREE diagnostics; only fix existing clauses.
